item.description:
# AI Usage Battery 0.2.4 (Build 8)
This release fixes the production menu-bar visibility bug, keeps Codex and Claude usage visible as token-aware meters, and hardens the direct-download DMG release path.
## Changes
- Replaces the SwiftUI `MenuBarExtra` anchor with an AppKit `NSStatusItem` so the app stays visible in the menu bar even when old macOS menu-bar visibility state is hidden. - Keeps the existing SwiftUI popover and settings UI while using AppKit only for the menu-bar item ownership. - Shows Codex and Claude usage with token-aware tracking and projected cost details from local usage data. - Preserves the account-aware usage detection fixes that prevent cached usage from bleeding across switched accounts. - Signs, notarizes, staples, and Gatekeeper-assesses the distributed DMG itself before release. - Blocks publishing when local ZIP or DMG release assets fail signature, notarization, or Gatekeeper checks. - Downloads the uploaded GitHub Release assets and compares SHA-256 digests against the local artifacts before updating the public appcast.
## Verification
- Swift test suite passes. - Debug Xcode build passes. - Debug AppKit status-item build was launched locally and appeared in the macOS menu bar while menu-bar settings toggles were off. - Release scripts now include hard gates for ZIP payload signature verification, DMG signing, DMG notarization, stapling, install assessment, and uploaded asset digest verification.
